Title: Volatile DMNT directly protects plants against Plutella xylostella by disrupting the peritrophic matrix barrier in insect midgut

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repression of PxMucin is associated with DMNT-induced peritrophic matrix disruption, reduced larval growth, survival, and pupation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; dsRNA-mediated RNAi feeding; histological section/HE staining; survival and pupation phenotyping
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Midgut; transcript decreased after DMNT treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DMNT treatment significantly downregulated PxMucin in P. xylostella larvae. 2. RNAi knockdown of PxMucin phenocopied DMNT treatment by thinning/disrupting the peritrophic matrix. 3. PxMucin knockdown reduced larval weight, survival, and pupation, indicating PxMucin is required for gut barrier integrity under DMNT challenge.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown impairs larval development and survival; no plant-side tradeoff reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports targeting insect gut barrier genes or exploiting DMNT to weaken pest peritrophic matrix integrity for crop protection.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors conclude DMNT may disrupt the peritrophic matrix partially through repression of PxMucin; not claimed as the sole mechanism. Homologs peritrophin 1-like (LOC105381868), chondroitin proteoglycan 2-like (LOC119693213), and mucin 2-like (LOC105395714) were checked as off-target controls and were not significantly affected by PxMucin dsRNA.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of whole P. xylostella larvae after 12 h DMNT treatment; 16S rDNA sequencing used in paper but not specific to PxMucin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural plant volatile involved in plant defense and insect repellence/toxicity

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Terpene synthase-related enzyme reported as key enzyme for DMNT biosynthesis in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of PEN1 increased DMNT accumulation in Arabidopsis and conferred enhanced resistance to P. xylostella.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 35S overexpression in Arabidopsis; GC-MS metabolite profiling; insect feeding bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene overexpressed in 35Spro:PEN1 plants; DMNT highly induced by P. xylostella infestation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEN1-overexpressing Arabidopsis plants accumulated more DMNT by GC-MS. 2. PEN1 transgenic plants showed stronger resistance and reduced larval survival of P. xylostella. 3. The paper treats PEN1 as a key enzyme responsible for DMNT biosynthesis linking plant gene activity to insect protection.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Promotes formation of the volatile homoterpene DMNT in Arabidopsis plants.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key enzyme for DMNT biosynthesis as stated in the paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P. xylostella infestation induces DMNT accumulation; long-day growth conditions for Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PEN1 overexpression or engineering DMNT biosynthesis could be used to enhance crop resistance to chewing insects.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense volatile that repels and directly kills P. xylostella larvae
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile emitted from aerial plant tissues/seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The manuscript states PEN1 is the key enzyme in DMNT biosynthesis and cites Sohrabi et al., 2015 for this assignment.
